content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Restricted Bag Policy - Wembley Stadium

  - url: https://www.wembleystadium.com/plan-your-visit/stadium-guide/restricted-bag-policy

  - source: Wembley Stadium

  - domain: wembleystadium.com


  ## Content


  # Bag policy


  Wembley Stadium restricts the bags that spectators and staff may bring into the
  stadium.


  ## What is allowed?


  Each person may bring one small bag. The bag must not be bigger than A4 size:


  - Height: 297 mm (11.7 inches)

  - Width: 210 mm (8.27 inches)

  - Depth: 210 mm (8.27 inches)


  The limit applies to the overall size of the bag. A larger bag will not be accepted
  simply because it is half full or folded over. Anyone carrying a bag that does not
  meet the stated criteria may be refused entry.


  Wembley says the policy is intended to improve safety and speed up security screening
  at the stadium.


  ## Merchandise and screening


  Matchday merchandise is supplied in a clear plastic bag. Merchandise bought outside
  the stadium from official retail locations may be placed in a sealed bag; spectators
  should keep the seal intact until they have entered.


  All items and bags carried by spectators and staff may be inspected on entry. Screening
  can include electronic checks or a pat-down. Bags are checked at an outer cordon,
  and a bag gauge may be used when there is a question about size.


  ## Medical exceptions


  Exceptions may be made for medical equipment and certain other items, but permission
  must be arranged in advance. Eligible visitors receive a certificate for the approved
  item and must show it to security. The bag may still be searched or x-rayed before
  entry.


  ## Handbags and personal devices


  Phones and cameras may be carried without a separate bag. Handbags count as the
  one bag allowed per person and must remain within the stated A4 dimensions.


  Wembley also notes that its restricted bag policy is different from the NFL Clear
  Bag Policy.

  '

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: NFL Stadium Game Day Bag Policy

  - url: https://www.nfl.com/legal/clear-bag-policy

  - source: NFL

  - domain: nfl.com


  ## Content


  # Clear Bag Policy


  NFL teams use a bag policy intended to improve public safety and speed up fan entry.
  The policy limits the size and type of bags that may be brought toward stadium gates
  and queue areas.


  Before attending a game, fans are encouraged to check the relevant team website
  for additional stadium-specific guidance.


  ## Permitted bag formats


  The policy permits clear plastic, vinyl or PVC bags that do not exceed:


  - 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches

  - 30.5 cm by 15.25 cm by 30.5 cm


  Clear backpacks, clear fanny packs and clear cinch bags can qualify if they meet
  the size rules. Hardware or decoration must not conceal part of the bag.


  A one-gallon clear plastic freezer bag is also permitted.


  A small non-clear bag may be carried if it does not exceed 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches.
  The policy describes this as an additional small bag that can accompany an approved
  clear bag.


  ## Non-compliant bags


  Examples of bags that do not meet the policy include non-clear backpacks, non-clear
  fanny packs, non-clear cinch bags, briefcases, luggage, computer bags and other
  non-clear bags larger than the permitted small-bag size.


  Fans carrying a bag that does not meet the criteria may be turned away before reaching
  the stadium gates.


  The policy says fans should check with the local stadium before the game to learn
  whether any alternative storage option is available.


  ## Medical items


  Exceptions may be made for medically necessary items after proper inspection. Security
  at stadium entrances may inspect bags and medical equipment covered by an exception.

  '
metadata: {}
title: NFL Stadium Game Day Bag Policy
url: https://www.nfl.com/legal/clear-bag-policy

Your **black backpack is unlikely to be admitted**.

- **Wembley’s venue baseline:** Your bag is within the A4-style limit of **29.7 × 21 × 21 cm**.  
- **NFL event policy:** The linked NFL policy for your **2026 NFL London Game at Wembley Stadium on 18 October 2026** is stricter. It permits:
  - A clear plastic/vinyl/PVC bag up to **30.5 × 15.25 × 30.5 cm**, or
  - A clear one-gallon freezer bag, or
  - A small **non-clear** bag no larger than **11.4 × 16.5 cm** (4.5 × 6.5 inches).

Your backpack is **non-clear and 26 × 18 × 12 cm**, so it exceeds the NFL small non-clear-bag limit. Non-clear backpacks over that size may be turned away before the gates. The NFL also notes that its policy differs from Wembley’s normal restricted-bag policy.

### Before you go

1. **Do not rely on the backpack being accepted.** Take no bag, or replace it with a compliant clear bag within the NFL dimensions.
2. **Check the official event guidance again near the event date**, since your ticket information says entry requirements may be updated.
3. **Keep your mobile tickets ready** and allow extra time for screening by Wembley stewards.
4. If you need to bring the backpack, **arrange storage in advance** rather than arriving with it. A commercial storage option is available in the Wembley area, but availability, booking and payment are separate arrangements and storage does not guarantee stadium admission.

Sources: [Wembley restricted-bag policy](https://www.wembleystadium.com/plan-your-visit/stadium-guide/restricted-bag-policy) and [NFL Clear Bag Policy](https://www.nfl.com/legal/clear-bag-policy).

### Before you go

1. **Do not rely on the backpack being accepted.** Take no bag, or replace it with a compliant clear bag within the NFL dimensions.
2. **Check the official event guidance again near the event date**, since your ticket information says entry requirements may be updated.
3. **Keep your mobile tickets ready** and allow extra time for screening by Wembley stewards.
4. If you need to bring the backpack, **arrange storage in advance** rather than arriving with it. A commercial storage option is available in the Wembley area, but availability, booking and payment are separate arrangements and storage does not guarantee stadium admission.
